compositional_truth_check.p4_silver_guest: Present (Shared with P2, showing signs of decline)

inventory_reconciliation.notes: P2 (Mexican Mint) is no longer viable; the specimen has undergone complete senescence. P3 remains the primary anchor.

plant_audit.p2: Deceased. Stem structure is non-existent; soil surface is barren of active growth.
plant_audit.p3: Pothos leaf remains turgid, though the surrounding soil shows signs of moisture fluctuation.
plant_audit.p4: Stunted growth; leaf tissue is thin and losing structural integrity.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-4: Initial observation of leaf senescence in P2.
temporal_deltas.t-4_to_t-2: Progressive browning and collapse of P2 foliage.
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_current: Complete loss of P2; P3 and P4 remain in a state of stasis with minimal growth.

anomalies: Presence of white granular debris (perlite) across all pots.
Electronic component (connector) visible in the lower left of the frame.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently experiencing a significant decline. While the String of Nickels (P1) maintains its baseline health, the Mexican Mint (P2) has suffered a total systemic failure. The Pothos (P3) remains the most resilient, though its growth is stagnant. The environment appears to be suffering from a lack of consistent hydration or nutrient uptake, as evidenced by the desiccated appearance of the soil and the loss of the P2 specimen.
